# Dexter v0.0.0 — Initial release

**Dexter** turns a single product photograph into an **articulated 3D asset** — separate part meshes, a kinematic tree, and a USD package loadable in [NVIDIA Isaac Sim](https://developer.nvidia.com/isaac/sim).

This is the first public release. The pipeline is end-to-end functional; APIs and schemas may evolve before v1.0.0.

## Highlights

- **Agentic pipeline** — OpenCode orchestrator with `analyze` and `critic` subagents, human gates, and a placement iteration loop.
- **Deterministic tooling** — Blender assembly, multi-view rendering, USD export, and schema-validated JSON IRs.
- **Reference assets** — Dishwasher, refrigerator, washing machine, and oven inputs in `input_images/`.
- **Documentation** — Nextra docs site covering architecture, sample runs, and contributor guides.
- **Sample outputs** — Real run artifacts on [Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/datasets/varmology/dexter-sample-outputs).

## Quick start

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
export OPENAI_API_KEY=...   # component PNGs
export FAL_KEY=...          # image-to-3D GLBs

opencode run --agent orchestrator -- "build the dishwasher from input_images/dishwasher.png"
```

## Requirements

Python 3.10+, Blender 3.6+, [OpenCode](https://opencode.ai), `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `FAL_KEY`. See the [requirements guide](https://github.com/aakashvarma/dexter/blob/main/docs/pages/getting-started/requirements.mdx).
